name: ANI
description: Asian News International — India's largest wire service. Frequently cited for pro-government sourcing and access journalism.

name: Al Jazeera
description: Qatar-based international broadcaster. Consistent focus on Global South and anti-Western-hegemony narratives. Strong on geopolitical coverage outside mainstream Western frames.

name: Bloomberg
description: Global financial news agency. Factual, market-focused, mild US-establishment lean. Strongest on financial and trade coverage.

name: Business Standard
description: Leading Indian financial daily. Generally factual with a business/market-oriented editorial lens. Low sensationalism.

name: BusinessToday
description: Indian business magazine and digital outlet. Centrist, personalises financial stories heavily for retail readers.

name: Council on Foreign Relations
description: US foreign policy think tank. Analytical, low sensationalism, broadly reflects mainstream US strategic establishment thinking.

name: Deccan Herald
description: Bengaluru-based broadsheet with national coverage. Mild liberal lean, generally factual, lower sensationalism than tabloids.

name: Free Press Journal
description: Mumbai-based daily. Centrist-right, heavy focus on local Mumbai news, higher sensationalism in weather and civic stories.

name: Hindustan Times
description: Delhi-based English daily. Mild centre-right lean, moderate sensationalism, broad national coverage.

name: India TV News
description: Hindi news channel with English digital presence. Strongly nationalistic framing, high sensationalism in defence and security coverage.

name: Indian Express
description: Mumbai-based broadsheet. Generally factual, mild liberal lean, strong investigative journalism tradition.

name: Mumbai Live
description: Mumbai-focused digital news outlet. Civic and local stories, centrist, moderate sensationalism.

name: NDTV
description: Delhi-based TV news channel. Generally opposition-leaning framing, moderate sensationalism.

name: News9Live
description: Indian digital news outlet. Centrist-right, strong on Mumbai and local civic stories, moderate sensationalism in headlines.

name: NewsOnAir (AIR)
description: All India Radio's digital news service — a government broadcaster. Consistently reflects official government positions, factual but editorially aligned with ruling party statements.

name: NewsX
description: Indian English news channel. Centrist-right, moderate-to-high sensationalism, heavy use of question-format clickbait headlines.

name: Outlook India
description: Indian newsmagazine with digital presence. Mild liberal lean, generally factual, moderate-length analysis pieces.

name: Republic World
description: Indian news channel founded by Arnab Goswami. Strong pro-government lean, high sensationalism, aggressive editorial style.

name: Reuters
description: International wire service. Consistently factual, neutral political framing, low sensationalism. Global reference baseline.

name: The Diplomat
description: International affairs magazine covering Asia-Pacific. Analytical and policy-focused, low sensationalism, mild Western-liberal lean.

name: The Federal
description: Indian digital outlet with focus on southern India. Liberal editorial lean, moderate sensationalism in headlines.

name: The Hindu
description: Chennai-based broadsheet. High factual standards, consistent left-liberal lean, low sensationalism.

name: The Print
description: Delhi-based digital outlet. Generally factual with a liberal-centrist lean. Known for policy analysis and commentary.

name: The Wire
description: Independent Indian digital outlet. Strong left-liberal editorial stance, focus on civil liberties and government accountability.

name: Times of India
description: India's largest English daily by circulation. Centrist-right, well-known for clickbait headlines and personalised 'what it means for you' framing.

name: Tribune India
description: Chandigarh-based broadsheet with national coverage. Generally factual, mild liberal lean, strong on parliamentary and political reporting.
